item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, though… Were they at least partially correct in that it *was* the cardboard boxes, and Declan/Hyacinth is behind it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Declan Hall does indeed have wider significance in the Gulf Canon, or at least in SCP-2472

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I quite liked this. It had a strange sort of low-key escalation to it that you typically don't see; it was also easy for me to imagine a bunch of researchers lost in their own increasingly narrow narrative, slipping deeper and deeper down a rabbithole of assumptions.

My only disappointment was that you didn't end it with some oblique reference to some "Site Director Declan Hall" ordering the attack on the site — just to introduce that tiny sliver of 'wait, WHAT?!' in the reader's mind. But that's a tiny gripe (plus, 'Declan Hall' might have significance outside of this SCP that I don't know about).

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Fair point. Thank you for the heads up.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: I have kept the Gulf canon link and the Site-81 link, but have removed the links to the GoIs - I suspect people can find those if they need them.

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright, so this article makes a ton of refrences that can easily be turned into in-line links to various hubs, etc. These include the MC&D Hub, The Gulf Hub, the UIU Hub, and Site-81 Dossier. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

15 August 2015 - further amendments to SUSEOCT (Southern United States Extranormal Organization Cooperation Treaty) on behalf of the Foundation and representatives of UIU and Marshall, Carter and Dark LLP

To this:

15 August 2015 - further amendments to SUSEOCT (Southern United States Extranormal Organization Cooperation Treaty) on behalf of the Foundation and representatives of UIU and Marshall, Carter and Dark LLP

Changing this:

Unscheduled visit on 4 January 2016 by Site Director Aktus, Site-81

To this:

Unscheduled visit on 4 January 2016 by Site Director Aktus, Site-81

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The researcher is trying to connect things, and making a conspiracy theory. They think that this "Order of the Hyacinth" is trying to attack/spy on them.

And they're correct.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: thanks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: thank you

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: tank you

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The researcher is trying to connect things, and making a conspiracy theory. They think that this "Order of the Hyacinth" is trying to attack/spy on them. More context is given in the next note.
Fun fact: Hyacinth was a guy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
According to Greek myth, Hyacinth was a lover of the god Apollo, suggesting a connection.

Hyacinth was a mortal man (not a god) loved by the Greek god Apollo. In the myths, Hyacinth died and the flowers were created and named after him because he was beloved by a god. But that's the myths. The actual IRL flowers are just flowers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But I still have a hard time understanding the meaning of this paragraph.
Senior Researcher Michaels has noted that the new supplier for the boxes was "Apollo Office Supplies Inc.". Could this company be related to the Order of the Hyacinth, responsible for the death of Director O'June? According to Greek myth, Hyacinth was a lover of the god Apollo, suggesting a connection. The infestation of Louisiana by water hyacinth is well-known, and several staff have since corroborated reports of increased growth of the plant in bodies of water near the site. Water hyacinth is also known as the Terror of Bengal. Please advise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is hyacinth: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyacinthus_orientalis

This is water hyacinth: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eichhornia_crassipes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: • I hope I can translate this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: could you please tell me the difference between hyacinth and water hyacinth

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, and I can see that the chunkiness is producing the wrong interpretation. The Site isn't reassigning staff (ie sending them elsewhere), it is keeping staff at the Site - it's cancelling their leave (ie holiday) unless that leave is essential (eg medical).

I'll have a think about how to re-word that to make it clearer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Noting that this could be a more serious situation than originally anticipated, we have cancelled all non-essential staff leave as a precaution.

Is that ever chunky wording… Suggest you change that so it reads better. "We have reassigned all non-essential staff", perhaps?

item.title: Re: Conspiracy!
item.content:encoded: That is indeed a mistake - it should read "The removal of personnel or objects". Now fixed, thanks for spotting.

item.title: Re: Conspiracy!
item.content:encoded:
The removal or personnel or objects

I think that first "or" is a mistake unless you meant something else?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks, A Vacuum - glad you liked it. Although, much like the other skips I'm referring to (especially mine), the jokey tone shouldn't be confused with harmlessness. Together this constitutes probably the most successful attack on the Foundation in some time.

In terms of "focussed" - that's just some British spelling coming out. I've amended it, rather than headcanon that the head of the Foundation's Louisiana outpost just happens to be non-American.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
if the anomaly is focussed so narrowly

I don't know if you intended to double the s; this seems like it could've been an intentional choice, though I feel safer just putting it here in case you didn't know.

I liked this. It was a short, entertaining thrill ride. I thought you were going to take it in a darker direction at the mention of internal segregation of Catholic personnel, but then it continued on as a fully comedic piece, which I think was probably the best decision you could've made. Showing the gradual progression of the site's personnel into a deeper state of naive paranoia was amusing. Presentation was almost universally excellent, and clinical tone was spot-on.

Very little else to add. It's a quick burst of fun that's good at what it does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a fun read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Watching them try and combat this from inside, knowing full well they're compromised, was a ride. Poor bastards. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now I want to know more about the Order of the Hyacinth.

Which is just what they expected me to do.
+1
fuck yeah, I've been waiting for this one!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's interesting to see how a slow boil mass panic/paranoia can affect a relatively isolated population like workers in a Foundation facility. It reminds me a little of SCP-1268. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mass paranoia as an scp… I like the idea. Reminds me of various events from history (the red scare and Japanese internment camps come to mind) in which the general public worked themselves into a frenzy over nothing and responded with extremely unnecessary force to a problem they invented in their own minds. Quite well written +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This comment is going to be filled with deeper crit and a bit more than just Wild Mass Guessing about what's going on when I don't have a mid-term tomorrow morning. Until then, know that a) The Gulf coming back is much appreciated, b) the references to several of your skips (cardboard boxes, hehe) are not going unloved, and c) is this the end game of the non-anomalous air coupler?

item.title: Conspiracy!
item.content:encoded:
You know that feeling when you are sure that you've jumped to the right conclusion, but no-one believes you? How often are you wrong? How often are you right?

I hope you enjoy reading this. It falls roughly into the Gulf Canon, so my apologies to writers in that canon if I've messed up any plans you had. It also ties together at least 5 skips, including 3 of mine, and those of Marcuse and Kate McTiriss.

Thanks very much to tretter, Bluemorpho, Shio, DrMagnus and dankaar for their thoughts and feedback. In particular to tretter for some good thoughts about the style - sorry that we didn't get to discuss them further, but I really wanted to steal this slot if I could! And sorry djkaktus for throwing a reference to your Director Aktus without asking!
